title: ICT Paper 2: Practical Exam Guide
subtitle: LRN International GCSE ICT 7927 - Paper 2 Practical (70 marks, approx. 2.5 hours)
intro: Paper 2 is a hands-on practical examination taken at a computer, set and marked by LRN - not a written or multiple-choice paper. You are given a set of source files and must produce real documents: format a report and insert a chart, import and query a database, and build a presentation, capturing screenshots in an Evidence Document as you go. It assesses AO2 (practical application) almost entirely, and covers syllabus Units 5 and 6, which the theory Paper 1 does not.

overview.label: Format
overview.value: Practical exam at a computer using supplied source files; you produce and print/submit documents.

overview.label: Duration
overview.value: Approx. 2 hours 30 minutes (May/June 2025 paper; the spec indicates 2 hours 15 minutes).

overview.label: Assessment focus
overview.value: Almost entirely AO2 - practical skills in real software, marked on the finished output.

overview.label: Coverage
overview.value: Syllabus Units 5 & 6 (Document Management, Processing & Productivity Tools; Advanced Data Handling & Web Development) - the practical half not tested in Paper 1.

overview.label: Conditions
overview.value: No internet or email access during the exam; every printout must carry your name, centre and candidate number.

components.name: Task 1 - Evidence Document
components.summary: Set up the file that collects proof of your work throughout the exam.

criteria.criterion: Open the evidence file and add your name, centre and candidate number to every page
Save it with the required name (e.g. EVIDENCE + candidate number)
Keep it open to paste required screenshots as you complete later tasks

components.name: Task 2 - Document production
components.summary: Edit a report to a corporate house style and add a chart.

criteria.criterion: Create, modify and apply paragraph styles consistently
Build a chart from CSV data with correct title, labels and axes
Place the chart neatly within the column width with correct spacing

components.name: Task 3 - Databases
components.summary: Import data and answer questions from it accurately.

criteria.criterion: Import CSV files as tables with the correct field data types
Create queries/calculations and screenshot the formula used
Sort, filter and present the required records

components.name: Task 4 - Presentation
components.summary: Produce a short, consistent slideshow from a supplied brief.

criteria.criterion: Create the required number of slides from the source file
Apply a uniform layout, master slide and formatting

components.name: Task 5 - Output & printing
components.summary: Deliver clean, correctly labelled printouts/files for assessment.

criteria.criterion: Ensure candidate details appear on every printout
Print or export the required documents and evidence
Mark drafts clearly and place final output in the assessment folder

tools: Word processor (e.g. Microsoft Word) - styles, headers/footers, inserting and sizing charts.
Spreadsheet software - creating charts from CSV data with correct labels.
Database software (e.g. Access) - importing CSVs, setting field types, queries and calculations.
Presentation software (e.g. PowerPoint) - master slides and consistent layout.

steps.title: 1. Learn your software's shortcuts cold
steps.detail: The clock is tight for 70 marks. Know how to apply and modify styles, insert charts, import CSVs and set field types without hunting through menus.

steps.title: 2. Practise the Evidence Document habit
steps.detail: Many marks depend on screenshots proving what you did. Rehearse taking a screenshot, pasting it into the evidence file, and labelling it - as a reflex after every task.

steps.title: 3. Master styles and house style
steps.detail: Create, modify and apply paragraph styles rather than formatting text manually. Consistent, style-based formatting is exactly what the document task rewards.

steps.title: 4. Get fluent with charts from data
steps.detail: Practise turning a CSV column into a correctly titled and labelled chart, sized to fit within a column - a recurring, easily-lost set of marks.

steps.title: 5. Drill database import and queries
steps.detail: Import CSVs with the correct data types, then build and screenshot the queries/formulas asked for. Getting field types wrong on import breaks everything downstream.

steps.title: 6. Always add candidate details first
steps.detail: Printouts without your name, centre and candidate number are not marked. Make adding them to headers/footers the very first thing you do in each file.

steps.title: 7. Rehearse full past papers to time
steps.detail: Work an entire practical paper end to end under exam conditions so file management, saving with the right names, and printing all become automatic.

submission: All required printouts/exported files, each showing your name, centre and candidate number.
The completed Evidence Document containing the required screenshots.
The saved database and presentation files with the specified filenames.
Draft printouts clearly crossed out so they are not assessed.
tips: No internet or email is allowed during the exam - everything must come from the supplied source files.
Save constantly and use the exact filenames requested; wrong or missing filenames lose marks.
Read each step fully before acting - tasks specify precise labels, spacing and layouts that are marked exactly.
The Practice Exam covers Paper 1 theory (Units 1-4); Paper 2 is hands-on, so the best preparation is repeated practice in the actual software.
